title: centaurea alba subsp. heldreichii
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. heldreichii, also known as White Knapweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and tall, erect stems. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and indigestion. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Centaurea alba subsp. heldreichii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by drying the plant material and then infusing it in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
